LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN MITCHELL'S MOTION TO MODIFY OR AMEND ORDER ON DEMURRER IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 1, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE MR. MITCHELL THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MR. MITCHELL MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 3, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. THE DEMURRER IS NOT AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S COMPUTERIZED REGISTER OF ACTIONS. DEFENDANTS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS AND THE COMPLAINT TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN AUGUST 8, 2013 AT 4:00 PM OR THE DEMURRER WILL BE OVERRULED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOHN MITCHELL's Amended Motion To Modify Or Amend Order On Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is Denied. The Order filed on November 18, 2013 accurately reflects the Court's ruling on the demurrer. The Order states the grounds for demurrer in compliance with CCP sec. 472d, as it specifies what facts need to be pled in order to state a cause of action. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOHN MITCHELL'S Motion To Modify Or Amend Order On Demurrer is continued to August 1, 2014 to give Mr. Mitchell the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Mitchell must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 3, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JOHN C. MITCHELL, TEL. NO. 264-1456, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. BARON L. MILLER OF MILLER AND MILLER, TEL. NO. 522-0500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS BARRELIER ASSOCIATES AND DON JOSE BARRELIER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the second cause of action, and sustained with leave to amend as to the first, third, and fourth causes of action. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff has alleged facts that Defendants owed a duty of care to Plaintiff as a third party to the real property transaction, and that Defendants breached this duty when they failed to advise their clients to read the inspection report and not to misrepresent or fail to disclose facts material facts regarding the transaction. (See First Amended Complaint, 63; see also Norman I. Krug Real Estate Investments, Inc. v. Praszker (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 35, 42.) Defendants' demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the first cause of action. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to plead facts giving rise to Defendants' legal duty to indemnify Plaintiff. Defendant's demurrers to third and fourth causes of action are sustained. Both causes of action allege facts regarding Defendants' intentional misrepresentation. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts giving rise to Defendants' negligent and intentional misrepresentation. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
